comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: SPIKE RESOLVED (AC1): anthropic_cache_instructions=True caches the Agent(system_prompt=...) kwarg for rebar's exact call shape. Empirical: call1 cache_write_tokens=14416, call2 (different user instruction) cache_read_tokens=14416/write=0 — full cache hit on the stable system prompt. ⇒ Implementation = AnthropicModelSettings(anthropic_cache_instructions=True [+ anthropic_cache_tool_definitions for agentic]) gated to anthropic providers; NO user-message CachePoint, NO RunRequest content-list change (BLOCKER 2 dissolved). Usage fields: result.usage.cache_read_tokens / cache_write_tokens (usage is a PROPERTY now, not a method).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DE-RISK E1 PASSED: anthropic_cache_instructions+tool_definitions caches the REAL container call shape (agentic + STRUCTURED output + tools). Empirical: call1 write=13923/read=0, call2 (different user instruction) write=0/read=13923 — full hit. S1 is technically de-risked end-to-end (structured-output path does NOT break caching).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DE-RISK A (INTEGRATION) PASSED: enabling anthropic_cache_instructions+tool_definitions on rebar's ACTUAL runner (monkeypatched) over a real 2-child epic review — the 8 container calls show 1 WRITE / 8 READS across the fan-out (reads grow 2644->3966->5288 as the cached prefix accumulates). The real rebar path caches end-to-end, not just an isolated pydantic-ai agent. (Note: this is the SEQUENTIAL loop, which already caches because calls are back-to-back; warm-then-fan-out (S3) is what preserves this once parallelized — per E2.)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent c81c plan-review fan-out epic; this child S1 reviewed whole against the full sibling roster]

4-bucket audit of parent acceptance/success criteria, mapping S1's contribution and cross-checking absences against the roster:

- "materially lower token cost" / "cache reads non-zero" — FULLY covered here (system-prompt + tool caching, AC: `usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0`; described as the biggest single token win).
- "caching provider-safe: openai/gemini succeed unchanged" — FULLY covered here (anthropic-gated via `_anthropic_cache_settings`; per-provider verification AC).
- "make lint + suite pass" — per-child portion covered here.
- "completes in minutes (wall-clock)" — PARTIAL here (caching is the cost lever); wall-clock delegated to sibling ba7e (S3 parallelize). Covered by roster.
- "caching across plan-review AND all workflow agent steps" — PARTIAL here (runner/plan-review seam only; engine RunnerAgentStep explicitly Out-of-scope -> sibling c6e5/S2). Covered by roster.
- "no fidelity regression / within offline-eval tolerance" — STRUCTURAL here (fidelity-neutral by construction); semantic eval gating -> sibling da34/S6. Covered by roster.
- "all six child stories closed" — aggregate/structural; not a single-child mapping.

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the union of children. G3: CLEAN — no findings for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: SPIKE RESOLVED (AC1). rebar's call shape is Agent(system_prompt=<parent plan>) + run_sync(<per-pairing instructions>) — instructions is the pydantic-ai user_prompt, NOT pydantic-ai 'instructions', so there are NO instruction_parts. In pydantic_ai 1.107.0 anthropic.py:1611-1616 the no-instruction-parts branch of anthropic_cache_instructions puts cache_control on cache_block_idx=0 = the system_prompt block DIRECTLY. => system-prompt caching IS supported for our shape; no instructions=/user-message CachePoint fallback needed. Implemented at the shared runner seam (runner.py): anthropic-GATED AnthropicModelSettings(anthropic_cache_instructions=True, anthropic_cache_tool_definitions=True) attached via kwargs (flows to text + structured + retry agents); gated on resolved.startswith('anthropic') so openai/gemini are unchanged. result.usage() captured via _extract_usage (RunUsage.cache_read_tokens/cache_write_tokens — pydantic-ai normalizes Anthropic's cache_read_input_tokens) and surfaced per run on result['_usage'] + the INFO log line. No RunRequest content change => structured-output retry path untouched (fidelity-neutral). Tests: gating (anthropic on / openai+gemini off), usage extraction (+defensive), wiring (settings reach Agent kwargs), container system-prompt byte-stability across children. Subagent code review: PASS (only out-of-scope NITs).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review [G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection]: PASS, no findings.

Grounded in src/rebar/llm/runner.py:209-303,421-435 and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:173-182,256-270.

Affirmations:
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: caching the SYSTEM PROMPT is the correct lever — the re-sent stable bytes (whole parent plan) are rendered into system_prompt via _resolve_system (passes.py:257), not the user message. anthropic_cache_instructions places cache_control on the system-prompt block for rebar's no-instruction-parts shape (runner.py:212-214). Retry path holds: _pai_structured rebuilds only the user `prompt`, passing `kwargs` (system_prompt + model_settings) unchanged (runner.py:368/372-391) — so caching survives the bounded retry exactly as the plan claims ("structured-output retry path untouched").
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: caches the actual re-sent bytes, not a proxy; AC#3 (cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on a repeat container call) is a real end-to-end proof the mechanism works, not a surrogate.
- APPROACH SELECTION: generated alternatives — (a) user-message CachePoint on the parent plan, (b) routing the stable plan through instructions= to cache there. (a) is defensibly worse (changes the content-list, perturbs the structured-retry path, and the stable bytes aren't even in the user message); (b) is the plan's own spike-contingent fallback. Chosen seam approach is at-least-as-good on blast-radius (no content-list change), codebase-alignment (single shared runner seam), and testability. No clearly-superior missed alternative — discarded.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present and strong: "the re-sent stable bytes ... live in the SYSTEM PROMPT ... so the correct, minimal lever is caching the SYSTEM PROMPT" plus the anthropic-gating rationale (keys error on openai/gemini).
- SPIKE-contingency is sound, not a defect: the mechanism is gated on a verifiable spike with a pre-committed documented fallback, so the approach is robust to either spike outcome.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization). Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child (S1) against the whole sibling roster (S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations:
- Implicit shared state / consumer impact: S1 owns the runner.py cache flags + `result.usage()` capture (the shared runner seam used by both passes.py and the engine RunnerAgentStep). S3 and S2 are downstream consumers (both assert `cache_read_input_tokens > 0`); the producer→consumer relation is DAG-ordered (S1->{S2,S3}). Anthropic gating (`resolved.startswith("anthropic")`) protects existing openai/gemini consumers of the shared runner.
- Conflicting assumptions: no sibling assumes the user-message-CachePoint path; all consume `anthropic_cache_instructions` + cache-token observability, which S1's body delivers. S1 aligns with the parent's Remediation ('cache the SYSTEM PROMPT … no RunRequest content-list change'), which supersedes the earlier epic-comment 'CachePoint-in-user-content' framing.
- Dependency gap / ordering: S1 is foundational (no deps); S2/S3 declare dependence; byte-stability (an S1 AC) is the precondition S3's warm-then-fan-out relies on (confirmed by S3's de-risk E2). Correctly ordered.
- Scope overlap: S1 fences out S2/S3/S4/S5 and each fences S1 reciprocally. Shared file runner.py is split cleanly — S1 owns the cache-flag mechanism, S2 owns RunnerAgentStep prefix-wiring.
- Residual references: S2's de-risk E3 reconciles that the container `{{plan}}` (stable, S1's domain) is distinct from the generic gate prompts' volatile `{{ticket_context}}` (S2's domain). No sibling carries a residual contract against the design S1 removed.

Non-G4 note (intra-child, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold, recorded for editorial cleanup only): the child TITLE still reads 'CachePoint + anthropic_cache_*' while the body explicitly rejects the user-message CachePoint in favor of system-prompt caching. This is a stale title label, not a cross-child inconsistency, and reads benignly under the ANTI-FP filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility overlay) — coaching findings + affirmations. The plan integrates pydantic-ai 1.107.0 anthropic prompt-caching capability, so T3 applies.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria passed):
- T3(a) technical_feasibility: the integration is achievable as described. The exact mechanism (AnthropicModelSettings(anthropic_cache_instructions=True, anthropic_cache_tool_definitions=True), anthropic-gated via resolved.startswith("anthropic"), applied at the shared runner seam with no RunRequest content-list change) is sound and matches a working implementation pattern (src/rebar/llm/runner.py:209-222, 421-435).
- T3(d) critical capability gap routes to SPIKE: the plan correctly front-loads a SPIKE ("SPIKE FIRST: confirm what pydantic-ai 1.107.0 anthropic_cache_instructions caches for rebar's call shape") to resolve the load-bearing unknown (does cache_control land on system_prompt= vs instructions=) before committing the implementation — exactly the right routing for a genuine capability uncertainty.
- T3(b) named API surface (settings keys): anthropic_cache_instructions / anthropic_cache_tool_definitions exist on AnthropicModelSettings — MATCH (verified at src/rebar/llm/runner.py:430-435; docs/experiments/workflow-remediation-pocs/runtime_pydanticai_poc.py:107 uses AnthropicModelSettings).

FINDING (T3(b), MISMATCH on a named library field the plan depends on):
- [ ] Correct the asserted RunUsage cache-token field names: AC "On a repeat anthropic container call ... usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0" and the "What" bullet ("pin the pydantic-ai 1.107.0 RunUsage field names") name cache_read_input_tokens. pydantic-ai 1.107.0 NORMALIZES Anthropic's raw cache_*_input_tokens to cache_read_tokens / cache_write_tokens on RunUsage — so the field the AC reads must be usage.cache_read_tokens (the *_input_tokens form is the raw Anthropic API field, not the RunUsage attr). Evidence: tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:322-324 ("pydantic-ai 1.107.0 normalizes Anthropic's raw cache_*_input_tokens to cache_read_tokens / cache_write_tokens on RunUsage"); src/rebar/llm/runner.py:416-417 reads getattr(u, "cache_read_tokens"/"cache_write_tokens"). criteria: [T3]. location: Acceptance Criteria line "usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0" + What bullet "pin the pydantic-ai 1.107.0 RunUsage field names". suggested_fix: change the AC assertion to `usage.cache_read_tokens > 0` and note the spike must pin the NORMALIZED RunUsage attr names (cache_read_tokens / cache_write_tokens), distinguishing them from Anthropic's raw cache_*_input_tokens.

Note (not a finding — auth/HTTPS, T3(c)): provider auth/transport is handled by the existing pydantic-ai provider seam already in use; no new auth/HTTPS precondition is introduced by adding model_settings flags, so T3(c) is not-applicable here.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 — T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness) review.

Affirmations (criteria passed):
- No undefined-but-referenced enum/schema vocabulary: every referenced symbol exists and is specified in-tree — `_resolve_system` (passes.py:173), `AnthropicModelSettings.anthropic_cache_instructions`/`anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` (runner.py:430-435), the anthropic gate `resolved.startswith("anthropic")` (runner.py:428), and `result.usage()`/RunUsage capture (runner.py:394-418). The structured output contract `plan_review_findings` is registered (passes.py:145) and unchanged by this plan.
- Fallback for a failed/incomplete sub-step IS specified: the load-bearing spike has an explicit documented fallback chain — system-prompt caching → route stable plan through `instructions=` → user-message `CachePoint` (deferred). AC1 ties the implementation to the recorded spike outcome.
- No ambiguous counter/state increment introduced; no new processing-protocol/schema co-location surface; no instruction-locality / pink-elephant risk (the plan explicitly makes NO user-message content-list change and NO prompt-text edit, so the structured-output retry path is untouched).

One grounded coaching item:

- [ ] AC3 names the field `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` but pydantic-ai 1.107.0 NORMALIZES Anthropic's raw `cache_read_input_tokens`/`cache_creation_input_tokens` to `cache_read_tokens`/`cache_write_tokens` (see `_extract_usage` docstring + capture, runner.py:397-418 citing usage.py:194-200). Reword AC3 to assert the normalized field (`usage.cache_read_tokens > 0`, surfaced as `cache_read_tokens`) so the executor's verification key matches what the code/library actually exposes.
  - criteria: T8
  - location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 ("usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0")
  - evidence: AC3 quoted phrase "usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0"; runner.py:413-417 returns `cache_read_tokens`/`cache_write_tokens`; runner.py:397-400 docstring "library NORMALIZES Anthropic's raw cache_read_input_tokens ... to cache_read_tokens / cache_write_tokens (usage.py:194-200)". This is the one referenced field-vocabulary mismatch an executor verifying AC3 would trip on. (The SPIKE AC1 already owns pinning RunUsage field names, which mitigates this — hence light, not blocking.)
  - suggested_fix: "On a repeat anthropic container call (same parent plan), `usage.cache_read_tokens > 0` (pydantic-ai normalizes Anthropic's raw cache_read_input_tokens to cache_read_tokens)."

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 (T1 prior-art/capability) review note: The plan's central capability question — "does pydantic-ai 1.107.0 anthropic_cache_instructions cache the system_prompt= kwarg?" — appears already RESOLVED and IMPLEMENTED in src/rebar/llm/runner.py. See _anthropic_cache_settings (runner.py:421-435) emitting AnthropicModelSettings(anthropic_cache_instructions=True, anthropic_cache_tool_definitions=True) gated on resolved.startswith("anthropic") (runner.py:428), the inline note at runner.py:213-214 ("anthropic.py:1611-1616, the no-instruction-parts branch caches the system prompt block directly"), usage capture via _extract_usage with cache_read_tokens/cache_write_tokens (runner.py:413-418), and result["_usage"] surfacing (runner.py:303). The plan should reconcile its "SPIKE FIRST" framing against this existing implementation rather than re-deriving it.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): CLEAN. This is a caching-infrastructure plan at the runner seam, not a plan that defines new LLM schemas/enums/sub-agents/protocols. Tool-verified: the SPIKE question ("does anthropic_cache_instructions cache system_prompt= or only instructions=?") is resolved in-code (runner.py:213-214 cites the anthropic.py:1611-1616 no-instruction-parts branch that caches the system-prompt block directly); the RunUsage field-name pin is concrete and correct (_extract_usage at runner.py:394-418 documents the library's NORMALIZATION of cache_read_input_tokens→cache_read_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens→cache_write_tokens at usage.py:194-200). The plan's fallback for the failed/incomplete sub-step (spike resolving "instructions-only") is explicitly specified (route stable plan through instructions=, else user-message CachePoint, deferred). No undefined-but-referenced enum/protocol, no ambiguous counter placement, no instruction-locality antipattern. No T8 findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c-cbcd-17a0-43d2 (plan-review fan-out optimization). Reviewed THIS child (S1) WHOLE against the complete sibling roster (S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations:
- Implicit shared state / consumer impact: S1 owns the runner.py cache flags + result.usage()/cache-token observability at the SHARED runner seam (used by passes.py and the engine RunnerAgentStep). S3 (ba7e) and S2 (c6e5) are downstream consumers — both assert cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on repeats. Producer→consumer is DAG-ordered (S1 blocks both per the dep links). Anthropic gating (resolved.startswith("anthropic")) protects existing openai/gemini consumers of the shared runner; no RunRequest content-list change protects the structured-output retry path.
- Conflicting assumptions: no sibling assumes the user-message CachePoint path. The parent Remediation ("cache the SYSTEM PROMPT … no RunRequest content-list change") supersedes the earlier CachePoint framing, and S2's de-risk E3 explicitly reconciles that the container {{plan}} is STABLE (S1's domain) vs generic gate prompts' volatile {{ticket_context}} (S2's domain). No contradiction.
- Dependency gap / ordering: S1 is foundational (no upstream deps); S2/S3 declare dependence. Byte-stability (S1 AC) is the precondition S3's warm-then-fan-out relies on, confirmed by S3 de-risk E2 (cold-concurrent => 3 writes/0 reads; warm-first => 0 writes/3 reads). Correctly ordered, no gap.
- Scope overlap: S1 fences out S2/S3/S4/S5 and each fences S1 reciprocally. Shared file runner.py is split cleanly — S1 owns the cache-flag mechanism; S2 owns RunnerAgentStep prefix-wiring/volatile-data relocation. No overlap.
- Residual references: no sibling carries a contract against a design S1 removed.

Non-G4 note (intra-child editorial only, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold): the child TITLE still reads "CachePoint + anthropic_cache_*" while the body explicitly REJECTS the user-message CachePoint in favor of system-prompt caching. Stale title label, not a cross-child inconsistency; reads benignly under the ANTI-FP filter. (Already noted on a prior pass; recorded for editorial cleanup, not a finding.)

compiled_state.description:
# Prompt caching at the runner seam (system-prompt + tool caching, provider-safe)

## Why
Across the container fan-out the re-sent stable bytes (the WHOLE parent plan) live in the SYSTEM PROMPT
(`passes.py:173` renders `parent_plan` into `_resolve_system`); `instructions` already holds only the volatile
per-pairing content. So the correct, minimal lever is caching the SYSTEM PROMPT (+ tool definitions on agentic
calls) — NOT a user-message `CachePoint`. Fidelity-neutral; biggest single token win. (Opus-review BLOCKER 1/2.)

## What
- SPIKE FIRST: confirm what pydantic-ai 1.107.0 `anthropic_cache_instructions` caches for rebar's call shape
  `Agent(system_prompt=...)` + `run_sync(instructions=...)` — does it apply `cache_control` to the `system_prompt=`
  kwarg, or only to `instructions=`? This decides the implementation. If it caches `system_prompt` → use it
  directly; if only `instructions=` → route the stable plan through `instructions=` (or fall back to a
  user-message `CachePoint`, deferred unless the spike requires it).
- Enable `anthropic_cache_instructions=True` + `anthropic_cache_tool_definitions=True` via `AnthropicModelSettings`,
  GATED to anthropic providers (`resolved.startswith("anthropic")`) — these keys error on openai/gemini. NO
  RunRequest prefix/suffix split and NO user-message content-list change (so the structured-output retry path
  `runner.py:347-363` is untouched — this dissolves BLOCKER 2).
- Capture `result.usage()` (currently discarded at `runner.py:226/343/356`) and surface cache_read/write input
  tokens per run (pin the pydantic-ai 1.107.0 RunUsage field names); thread into observability/coverage.
- Note: `anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` is a no-op on single_turn calls (tools=[]); only agentic calls benefit.

## Scope
- In: the spike; AnthropicModelSettings cache flags + anthropic gating in runner.py; `result.usage()` capture +
  cache-token observability; byte-stability of the container system prompt across pairings.
- Out: engine RunnerAgentStep caching (S2); parallelization/warm-up (S3); merge (S4); bin-pack (S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SPIKE resolved + recorded; the implementation follows it (system-prompt caching if supported, else the
      documented fallback).
- [ ] `anthropic_cache_instructions` + `anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` are enabled and GATED to anthropic
      providers; on openai/gemini the call succeeds unchanged (no cache_* sent) — verified via the per-provider
      message mapper (offline) or an explicit live/integration test.
- [ ] On a repeat anthropic container call (same parent plan), `usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0`.
- [ ] The container system prompt is byte-stable across pairings — a stability test asserts `_resolve_system`
      output is identical for different children of the same parent.
- [ ] `result.usage()` cache_read/write input tokens are captured and surfaced per run (observability).
- [ ] No behavior/finding change (fidelity-neutral); ≤3 cache breakpoints; `make lint` + tests pass.

file_impact.reason: AnthropicModelSettings cache flags + anthropic gating + result.usage() capture
confirm container system prompt carries the stable plan + is byte-stable

compiled_state.title: Prompt caching at the shared runner seam (CachePoint + anthropic_cache_*, provider-safe)
